rules: NEVER remove or edit features. ONLY the 'passes' field may be changed, and ONLY after the listed steps were executed end-to-end in this session. Removing or editing features is unacceptable because it could lead to missing or buggy functionality. NOTE (BRIEFING.md): Layer 4 features F25-F28 are deferred until ALL Phase 1-5 features pass, regardless of phase number.

features.description: Environment loader validates all required env vars and fails fast with readable errors
steps: Delete one required var from .env
Start the app
Verify it exits with an error naming the missing var
Restore the var and verify clean startup

features.description: Sui GraphQL client fetches a real testnet package with modules and disassembly (NEVER JSON-RPC)
steps: Call fetchPackage with a known testnet package id
Verify >= 1 module returned with non-empty disassembly
Verify version and upgradeCount fields populated
Run: grep -ri 'jsonrpc' src/ and verify ZERO matches

features.description: Invalid or missing packages produce typed errors, not crashes
steps: Call fetchPackage with malformed address, verify rejection before any network call
Call fetchPackage with a valid-format but nonexistent address
Verify PackageNotFoundError is thrown with a readable message

features.description: MVR reverse resolution converts package id to human-readable name, and never blocks an audit
steps: Resolve a known MVR-registered mainnet package, verify a name like @scope/name returns
Resolve an unregistered package, verify { name: null } returns without throwing
Simulate endpoint timeout, verify audit pipeline continues with nulls

features.description: Move source upload path builds a valid PackageContext from pasted files
steps: POST source files via the API
Verify non-.move files and >1MB payloads are rejected with 400
Verify a valid upload produces a PackageContext with modules populated

features.description: Finding and AuditReport zod schemas plus rule_id registry exist with 13 sector prefixes (ML-ACC, ML-INT, ML-HOT, ML-OWN, ML-ARI, ML-UPG, ML-RAC, ML-RET, ML-TOK, ML-WRP, ML-DOS, ML-DEP, ML-LOG) and 93 seeded rules
steps: Verify rules.ts contains all REGEX+AST rules parsed from movelens_vuln_corpus_classified.md across the 13 sector prefixes
Verify Finding schema rejects an unknown rule_id
Verify AuditReport schema requires the exact watermark string

features.description: Four Move test fixtures exist with expected.json ground truth
steps: Verify vulnerable_cap.move, missing_signer.move, overflow.move, clean.move exist in test/fixtures
Verify each has a sibling expected.json listing required rule_ids
Verify each fixture is under 80 lines and contains a module declaration

features.description: Layer 1 deterministic rule engine runs all 93 rules and emits schema-valid findings with confidence=1.0
steps: Run Layer 1 on vulnerable_cap.move, verify expected rule_ids appear with confidence 1.0 and source layer1
Run on missing_signer.move and overflow.move, verify expected rule_ids appear
Run on clean.move, verify zero critical or high findings
Verify every emitted finding validates against the zod schema

features.description: Engine is fault-tolerant and meets the time budget (Layers 1+2 under 5s, full run under 90s)
steps: Artificially make Layer 4 sidecar unreachable, verify audit completes with Layer 1+2 findings
Time Layers 1+2 on a fixture, verify under 5 seconds
Verify duplicate findings (same rule_id+module+line) are deduplicated keeping highest confidence

features.description: Layer 2 OZ benchmark runs 10 deviation checks and Cetus ML-OZ-001 always appears first in report
steps: Run Layer 2 on overflow.move
Verify ML-OZ-001 fires with severity critical and confidence 0.95
Verify the Cetus-class finding sorts first in the report
Verify all 10 OZ rules are implemented and unit-tested against deviation snippets

features.description: Report assembler produces a complete AuditReport with risk grade, sorted findings, and hardcoded watermark
steps: Assemble a report from fixture findings
Verify findings sorted critical-first, confidence descending within severity
Verify severity counts and A-F risk grade computed per the documented mapping
Verify watermark string present verbatim

features.description: Seal threshold encryption round-trips a report for the owner identity
steps: Encrypt a fixture report with encryptReport
Verify ciphertext is not parseable JSON
Decrypt with the owner identity, verify byte-identical report recovered
Disable Seal endpoint, verify pipeline falls back to plaintext with sealed=false and a loud warning (fallback works but this feature only passes with real Seal encryption)

features.description: Quilt bundler packages report.json, findings.enc, and summary.md into quilt entries
steps: Build a quilt from a fixture report
Verify 3 entries with correct identifiers
Verify report.json entry contains only public metadata plus watermark, not decrypted findings

features.description: Audit quilt uploads to Walrus testnet and returns a real blob id (CORE TRACK REQUIREMENT)
steps: Run a fixture audit end-to-end and upload
Verify a real blob id is returned and record it in progress.txt
Fetch the blob back by id from a fresh process and verify report.json matches
Wait several minutes, fetch again, verify still retrievable

features.description: Demo Move package (<=150 lines) is published on testnet and registered in MVR
steps: Build and publish the move/ demo module to testnet
Verify the package id on a Sui explorer
Register it in MVR testnet and verify the PackageInfo object is owned by our keypair

features.description: set_metadata attaches the Walrus blob id to our package on-chain and reads back
steps: Call attachAuditToPackage with a real blob id
Verify tx digest returned and record it in progress.txt
Call readAuditMetadata and verify the same blob id returns via GraphQL

features.description: AuditMemory abstraction layer with noop fallback keeps the pipeline alive when MemWal is disabled or down
steps: Set MEMWAL_ENABLED=false
Run a full fixture audit end-to-end, verify success with noop stub
Verify a warning is logged and memory_context_used is false

features.description: MemWal remember/recall loop: past findings are stored and injected into future agent prompts
steps: Audit a fixture, verify remember fires for findings with confidence >= 0.8
Audit a similar fixture, verify recall returns the stored pattern
Verify the recalled pattern appears in the agent prompt (via logs)
Verify report.memory_context_used is true

features.description: Audit API: POST starts an async job, GET polls status through all pipeline stages
steps: POST a fixture source, verify auditId returned immediately
Poll GET and observe status progressing through fetching/auditing/uploading to done
Verify a failed stage produces status failed with a human-readable error

features.description: Landing page accepts a package address or pasted source with client-side validation
steps: Open the app in a real browser
Type an invalid address, verify inline error and no API call
Paste fixture source in the source tab and submit
Verify navigation to /audit/[id]

features.description: Report page: live 12-step pipeline stepper, then full findings view with trust panel
steps: Run an audit from the browser and watch the stepper animate through stages
Verify finished report shows risk grade, severity chips, expandable findings with rule_id, line range, recommendation, confidence bar
Verify trust panel shows Walrus blob id link, Seal badge, MVR tx digest link
Verify watermark visibly rendered at top
Hard-refresh mid-audit and verify state recovers

features.description: Failure honesty: every stage failure surfaces a readable error, low-confidence findings are de-emphasized
steps: Force a Walrus upload failure, verify report page shows the error (no infinite spinner)
Verify findings with confidence < 0.4 are visually de-emphasized and labeled low confidence

features.description: Demo assets: demo.md script with pre-recorded backup blob id and tx digest, plus README
steps: Verify scripts/demo.md exists with the 3-minute flow mapped to judging criteria
Verify it contains a real backup blobId and tx digest from a pre-run audit
Verify README.md covers pitch, 12-step architecture, stack, setup

features.description: Cold-start integrity: fresh clone to working audit in under 5 minutes of human effort
steps: Clone to a clean directory
cp .env.example .env and fill keys
Run ./init.sh and verify health checks pass
Run a fixture audit from the browser and verify a report with blob id renders

features.description: LanceDB corpus seeded with 200 known-vulnerable Move snippets and similarity search returns correct match for Cetus pattern
steps: Run scripts/seedLanceDB.ts
Verify lancedb_store/ directory exists
Query with a Cetus-like snippet and confirm similarity > 0.75 returned

features.description: Layer 4 Python sidecar starts on port 8765 and /embed + /classify endpoints respond correctly
steps: pip install -r requirements.txt
python scripts/layer4_server.py
POST /embed with Cetus snippet → similar_to: cetus_checked_shlw
POST /classify with overflow snippet → vulnerable: true, category: ML-INT

features.description: Layer 4 TypeScript caller fetches sidecar results and merges confidence scores into final findings
steps: Run full audit on overflow.move fixture
Verify Layer 4 findings appear with source: layer4
Verify confidence score is composite (embed + classify + optional groq)
Verify Model C (Groq) only called when confidence is 0.4-0.7

features.description: Full 4-layer engine runs end-to-end on all fixtures under 90 seconds with no paid API calls
steps: Run engine on all 4 fixtures
Verify total time < 90s
grep -ri 'anthropic\|openai_api_key\|callClaude' src/ returns ZERO matches
Verify findings from all 4 layers present in output
